top_categories.workout_context: Vuelta Altas Cumbres' Sierras de Córdoba (Sierras Grandes/Traslasierra) mountain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 75 miles.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Vuelta Altas Cumbres' demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before March.
84ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
6,330ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
At Vuelta Altas Cumbres, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Vuelta Altas Cumbres. Train it until it feels boring.
75 miles on sierras de Córdoba mountain gravel (not the Andes burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Vuelta Altas Cumbres, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
6,330ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
Base fitness for Vuelta Altas Cumbres. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on sierras de Córdoba mountain gravel (not the Andes. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Double-threshold training builds sustained power with less fatigue. More FTP per hour of training.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for Vuelta Altas Cumbres’s 75 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.
race_overlay.altitude: Vuelta Altas Cumbres with 6,330ft of climbing, much of it above 8,000ft demands altitude preparation. Arrive 5–7 days early for acclimatization. Expect 5–15% power reduction at altitude. Increase iron intake 4 weeks out. Hydrate aggressively — altitude increases fluid loss by 20–40%. High altitude.
race_overlay.terrain: Highly technical terrain at Vuelta Altas Cumbres demands practice on similar surfaces. Ride sierras de córdoba mountain gravel (not the andes -- a separate, older range in central argentina) at race-day cadence weekly. Expect: sierras de córdoba (sierras grandes/traslasierra) mountain gravel, south american gravel, pampa de achala high plateau. Practice cornering, descending, and power delivery on unstable surfaces. Dial in tire pressure before race week — 5 PSI wrong costs you 15+ minutes over 75 miles.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Gravel Specific, Race Simulation, and TT Threshold to prepare you for 75 miles of sierras de córdoba mountain gravel (not the andes -- a separate, older range in central argentina) in Villa Cura Brochero, Córdoba Province, Argentina.
